title: Jurodedu
description: A table sink(-ish) modpack revolving mostly around both having fun and an operational computer.
body:
<!--TODO: Add more images.-->
# Jurodedu
_A modpack for 1.20.1 revolving around both having fun and an operational computer._

## NOTE!!
## The Fabric/1.20.1 version (v2) is **DEPRECATED!**
## It remains here only for archival purposes and may be removed for organization.
## You can find the old Jurodedu v2 code [here](https://github.com/FormunaGit/Jurodedu)

## What is this?
Jurodedu is a dual-sided modpack; it **must** run on both the server and the client. If you want to host it yourself, but lack the system horsepower, **e4mc** is bundled with it, just `Open to LAN` and share the IP that pops up with any friends around the world!
> Note that I would still recommend using a dedicated server, but if you don't have enough RAM, one instance of Minecraft is always lighter than two...

## What's in this?
As of v3.0.0 (The "Neoforged" Update), the modpack mostly revolves on the Create mod and generally any mod you can find

For right now at least, Jurodedu aims to be a very "this isn't very vanilla" collection. Veil Origins, Chipped, Create, Skin Restorer (for all the... not so legal players... or you just want to change your skin) and more come pre-installed!
> NOTE: One player reported crashing due to the Skin Restorer mod. If you own Minecraft or have another way of showing your skin, uninstall the Skin Restorer mod if you crash.

## My PC's gonna get fried, 100+ mods?!
![title screen shows 135 mods](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/abf7a51d2043e29186911f603f3e9f3f26729665.png)
Relax! Most of the mods are either libraries or optimization mods! I'm able to get 60+ FPS on 10 chunk-render distance on 16 gigabytes of RAM, so you (hopefully) should be fine.

Besides there are actually only ~70+ mods, according to Mod Menu...

![mod menu shows 76 mods](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1c6cfa69f36ebfd843493f01d18d3afef363ab43.png)

...so do what you will with that information.

## Why the name Jurodedu?
The name "Jurodedu" is a [corruption](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Language_change) of two words run through a Caesar cipher.

## Credits to...
- [Packwiz](https://packwiz.infra.link/), for their awesome CLI tool
- [Fabulously Optimized](https://modrinth.com/modpack/fabulously-optimized), for their [GitHub Workflow](https://github.com/Fabulously-Optimized/fabulously-optimized/blob/main/.github/workflows/auto-publish.yml), which I used as a template. (hope that's okay..?)
